tasks.title: Neon DB connection + Drizzle migrations
tasks.description: Configure drizzle.config.ts for the glass_atlas Postgres schema. Add db:generate, db:migrate, db:studio scripts to package.json. Verify src/lib/server/db/index.ts uses DATABASE_URL via $env/dynamic/private (already in place — confirm and document). Add a citation_events table to schema.ts (columns: id serial PK, note_slug text NOT NULL, cited_at timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()) — this lightweight counter records each time a note is retrieved by the chat RAG pipeline and powers the landing page 'total citations served' stat. Generate the initial migration from the existing schema.ts, then run it against Neon. The pgvector extension must be enabled in the Neon console before migrating. All tables must use the glass_atlas schema prefix (pgSchema('glass_atlas') in Drizzle).
acceptance_criteria: npm run db:generate produces migration files under drizzle/
npm run db:migrate applies the migration against Neon without errors
All glass_atlas schema tables exist in Neon (notes, citation_events, conversations, messages, auth tables)
pgvector extension is enabled on the Neon project
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Auth.js GitHub OAuth + admin route guard
tasks.description: Configure SvelteKitAuth in src/auth.ts with the GitHub provider using AUTH_GITHUB_ID and AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET env vars. Sessions are stateless JWT — no DrizzleAdapter needed. Implement hooks.server.ts to intercept all requests to /admin/** and redirect unauthenticated users to the GitHub OAuth flow. Read session from event.locals.session only — never read cookies manually. AUTH_TRUST_HOST must NOT be set (Railway deployment, not Vercel).
acceptance_criteria: GET /admin redirects to GitHub OAuth login when no session exists
After completing GitHub OAuth, session is available in event.locals.session
GET /admin loads successfully when authenticated
Public routes (/, /notes, /api/chat) are unaffected by the auth guard
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Root layout + global nav shell
tasks.description: Update src/routes/+layout.svelte to render Nav.svelte and a <slot /> (or {@render children()} in Svelte 5). Create src/lib/components/Nav.svelte: Glass Atlas logo centered, NOTES and CHAT links on the left, search icon (non-functional placeholder — wired in PUBLIC-03) + login-or-logout link + dark mode toggle on the right. Dark mode toggle: reads prefers-color-scheme on first load, persists preference to localStorage, and applies a .dark class to <html>. Update src/app.css to add a .dark selector block mirroring the existing @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) tokens so the class-toggle approach works. Match the nav design from reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Landing dark.png and Landing light.png.
acceptance_criteria: Nav renders logo, left nav links, and right controls on all pages
Dark mode toggle switches themes and the choice persists on reload
Login link shows GitHub login when unauthenticated; logout link when authenticated
Nav layout matches the design reference in both light and dark modes
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Notes DB query layer — CRUD helpers
tasks.description: Implement core CRUD helpers in src/lib/server/db/notes.ts. Functions: listNotes(filter?: { topic?: string, status?: 'draft'|'published', q?: string }), getNoteBySlug(slug: string), createNote(data: CreateNoteInput), updateNote(slug: string, data: UpdateNoteInput), deleteNote(slug: string). All functions must return typed plain objects — never raw Drizzle ORM objects. The q param in listNotes filters notes where title ILIKE '%q%' OR any element of tags ILIKE '%q%'. Export corresponding TypeScript types (Note, CreateNoteInput, UpdateNoteInput). Leave stub exports for searchNotesBySimilarity, recordCitations, and getTotalCitations — these are implemented in ADMIN-01b.
acceptance_criteria: listNotes() returns Note[] sorted by publishedAt desc
getNoteBySlug() returns Note or null
createNote() inserts and returns the created note
updateNote() patches specified fields and returns the updated note
deleteNote() removes the row
listNotes() filters by q with case-insensitive title and tag matching when provided
All return types are typed plain objects, no raw Drizzle types leaked
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Notes DB query layer — similarity search + citations
tasks.description: Extend src/lib/server/db/notes.ts (built in ADMIN-01a) with the advanced query functions. Functions: searchNotesBySimilarity(embedding: number[], limit: number), recordCitations(slugs: string[]): Promise<void> (bulk-inserts one citation_events row per slug), getTotalCitations(): Promise<number> (returns COUNT(*) from citation_events). Similarity search uses Drizzle's sql template tag for the pgvector cosine distance operator (<=>). All functions must return typed plain objects.
acceptance_criteria: searchNotesBySimilarity() returns notes ordered by cosine distance using the <=> operator
recordCitations() bulk-inserts one citation_events row per slug passed
getTotalCitations() returns the total COUNT(*) from citation_events
All return types are typed plain objects, no raw Drizzle types leaked
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Admin dashboard + notes list page
tasks.description: Create src/routes/admin/+page.server.ts: load function calls listNotes() with no filter to return all notes. Create src/routes/admin/+page.svelte: table-style list of notes with columns for note number, title, status badge (DRAFT/PUBLISHED), publishedAt date, edit link (/admin/notes/[slug]/edit), and a delete form action. Include a 'New Note' CTA button linking to /admin/notes/new. Add a DELETE form action that calls deleteNote(slug). Match the admin surface rules from docs/styleguide.md (Section 2 Admin).
acceptance_criteria: GET /admin lists all notes with correct columns
Status badge correctly shows DRAFT or PUBLISHED
Edit link navigates to /admin/notes/[slug]/edit
Delete action removes the note and refreshes the list
New Note button navigates to /admin/notes/new
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: MarkdownEditor component (CodeMirror 6)
tasks.description: Create src/lib/components/MarkdownEditor.svelte. Use Svelte 5 $props() for: value (bindable string), placeholder (optional string). Initialize CodeMirror EditorView in onMount with the markdown() language extension. Sync edits back to parent via an updateListener extension that calls a callback prop on every doc change. Teardown EditorView in onDestroy. Style the editor surface using --color-surface-2 background in light mode and --color-surface-1 in dark mode (matching the admin design reference blueprint panel recipe from docs/styleguide.md Section 5.6). Do not implement wiki-link autocomplete in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Editor renders in both light and dark modes with correct surface colors
Typing updates the bound value string
Editor initializes with the provided value on mount
onDestroy cleans up the EditorView instance
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Admin note create page
tasks.description: Create src/routes/admin/notes/new/+page.server.ts and +page.svelte. Layout: two-column matching reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Admin note editor dark.png — left column (title input, takeaway input, MarkdownEditor for body, tags multi-input, category select from note-taxonomy.ts), right sidebar (publishedAt date picker, status selector, series input, cover media URL input that maps to the `image` column — author can paste a JPEG/PNG/SVG/GIF/MP4 URL directly). Form action: create — slug auto-generated from title via src/lib/utils/slugify.ts. After create, redirect to the edit page for the new slug. First-party upload control wiring is handled in ADMIN-07.
acceptance_criteria: New note form creates a note and redirects to its edit page
Slug is auto-generated from the title via slugify.ts
All left-column and right-sidebar fields are present and functional
Cover media URL input saves to the `image` column
Two-column layout matches the admin design reference
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Admin note edit page + top bar
tasks.description: Create src/routes/admin/notes/[slug]/edit/+page.server.ts and +page.svelte. Load existing note data into all fields using the same two-column layout as ADMIN-04a. Top bar: breadcrumb showing note title, DRAFT/PUBLISHED status badge, PREVIEW button (links to /notes/[slug]), SAVE DRAFT button, PUBLISH button. Form actions: update (patches fields, preserves status), publish (sets status to published). Match reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Admin note editor dark.png. First-party upload control wiring is handled in ADMIN-07.
acceptance_criteria: Edit form loads existing note data into all fields
Save Draft updates the note without changing status
Publish action sets status to published
Breadcrumb shows note title and current status badge
PREVIEW button links to /notes/[slug]
Top bar and two-column layout match the admin design reference
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Embedding generation on note save
tasks.description: Implement src/lib/server/embeddings.ts exporting embedText(text: string): Promise<number[]>. Call the OpenRouter embeddings endpoint (POST to the OpenAI-compatible /embeddings route) with the note body text. In the ADMIN-04a create and ADMIN-04b update form actions, call embedText(body) after saving the note row and update the note's embedding column. If the OpenRouter call fails, log the error but do not block the save — the note saves with a null embedding. Read OPENROUTER_API_KEY from $env/dynamic/private.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a note generates and stores a non-null embedding in the DB
Editing a note body regenerates the embedding
If the OpenRouter call fails, the note saves successfully with a null embedding and the error is logged
OPENROUTER_API_KEY is read from env, never hardcoded
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: NoteCard component + notes browse page
tasks.description: Create src/lib/components/NoteCard.svelte: displays note number (zero-padded to 3 digits, e.g. 042), publishedAt date, tag badges, title, excerpt (first ~150 chars of body), and a READ NOTE link to /notes/[slug]. If `note.image` is set, render it above the excerpt using an `` tag (aspect-ratio: 16/9, object-fit: cover). When no image is set, render the row without any placeholder. See styleguide.md Section 6 for cover media rules. Match the note row design from reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Landing dark.png 'latest field notes' section — row-separated layout, no rounded cards. Create src/routes/notes/+page.server.ts: load all published notes, accept optional ?topic= query param for filtering via listNotes(). Create src/routes/notes/+page.svelte: page heading 'The latest field notes.', filter bar with topic select and sort controls, then a list of NoteCard rows.
acceptance_criteria: Published notes appear in reverse-chronological order
?topic= query param filters the list to matching notes
NoteCard renders number, date, tags, title, excerpt, and read link
Empty state is shown when no notes match the filter
Row layout with dividers matches the design reference
NoteCard renders cover image above the excerpt when `note.image` is set
NoteCard renders correctly with no cover image (no placeholder)
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Note detail page — server + Markdown rendering
tasks.description: Create src/routes/notes/[slug]/+page.server.ts: load the note by slug (404 if not found or not published), load all published notes for the left sidebar catalog, derive 3 related notes (same topic or category). Create the initial src/routes/notes/[slug]/+page.svelte scaffold: render the note title, cover image (if `note.image` is set, render it as a full-width `` with aspect-ratio: 16/9 and object-fit: cover above the title), and the full note body rendered as Markdown using unified with remark-gfm and rehype-shiki for syntax-highlighted code blocks. Return 404 for unpublished or unknown slugs. Three-column layout and sidebars are wired in PUBLIC-02b. See styleguide.md Section 6 for cover media rules.
acceptance_criteria: Note body renders as formatted Markdown with syntax-highlighted code blocks
Visiting an unpublished or non-existent slug returns a 404 response
Cover image renders full-width above the note title when `note.image` is set
Note detail renders correctly with no cover image (no placeholder)
Page loads the note, all published notes, and 3 related notes in the server load function
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Note detail page — three-column layout + sidebars
tasks.description: Extend src/routes/notes/[slug]/+page.svelte (built in PUBLIC-02a) with the full three-column layout from reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Note viewer dark.png: left sidebar (NEW CONVERSATION CTA button at top linking to /, then a scrollable list of all published notes by date with the current note highlighted), main column (cover image + title + Markdown body from PUBLIC-02a, already in place), right sidebar (OTHER NOTES section with 3 related note links, CITE THIS NOTE section with note slug/date/URL). Ensure layout matches the design reference in both light and dark modes.
acceptance_criteria: Left sidebar lists all published notes with the current note visually highlighted
NEW CONVERSATION button links to /
Right sidebar shows 3 related notes and cite information
Three-column layout matches the note viewer design reference
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: OpenRouter adapter + personality block + RAG assembly
tasks.description: Implement src/lib/server/ai/openrouter.ts: a streaming fetch client for OpenRouter's OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint. Use native fetch, not the openai SDK. Implement src/lib/server/personality.ts: export a SYSTEM_PROMPT string that instructs the LLM to answer only from retrieved note context, cite note slugs, and decline off-topic questions with 'I don't have a note on that.' Implement src/lib/server/chat.ts: export assembleContext(query: string): Promise<{ context: string; citedSlugs: string[] }> which calls searchNotesBySimilarity from notes.ts with a query embedding, formats the top-N results as 'Takeaway: ... First paragraph: ...' snippets (never full bodies), assembles the final prompt string, and returns it alongside the list of retrieved note slugs so the caller can record citations. Chat responses must stream via ReadableStream.
acceptance_criteria: openrouter.ts sends a valid streaming request to OpenRouter and returns a ReadableStream
personality.ts exports a non-empty SYSTEM_PROMPT string
chat.ts assembleContext() returns { context: string; citedSlugs: string[] } from top-N similar notes
Full note bodies are never passed to the LLM — only takeaway and first paragraph
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Chat API endpoint with IP-based rate limiting
tasks.description: Create src/routes/api/chat/+server.ts handling POST requests with body { message: string }. Rate limiting: maintain an in-memory Map keyed by a SHA-256 hash of the requester's IP address (read from request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') or getClientAddress()). Each entry tracks { count: number, windowStart: number }. On each request, if count >= 10 within the current 1-hour window, return a 429 Response with JSON { error: 'Rate limit exceeded' }. Otherwise increment count and call assembleContext(message) from chat.ts to get { context, citedSlugs }, then immediately call recordCitations(citedSlugs) from notes.ts (fire-and-forget — do not await, do not block the stream on it). Pass context to the streaming OpenRouter call. Return new Response(stream, { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream' } }). Never use SvelteKit's json() helper for this endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/chat returns a text/event-stream response that streams tokens
The 11th request within a 1-hour window from the same IP returns HTTP 429
The requester IP is hashed with SHA-256 before use as the rate-limit key
The response uses new Response(stream), not json()
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Chat.svelte component
tasks.description: Create src/lib/components/Chat.svelte using Svelte 5 runes. Props via $props(): compact (boolean, defaults to false). State: messages $state([]), input $state(''), loading $state(false), error $state(''), onSubmit handler POSTs to /api/chat using fetch + ReadableStream (not EventSource — POST body is required). While awaiting the first token, show an optimistic 'Searching notes…' message. Stream tokens into the last assistant message as they arrive. On 429, show 'Rate limit reached — try again in an hour.' Chat history lives in component $state only — it is never persisted and clears on page reload. Compact mode constrains the height for use in the landing hero panel. Matches the chat panel design from reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Landing dark.png. Input placeholder: 'Ask anything grounded in these notes…'
acceptance_criteria: Typing a message and submitting sends a POST to /api/chat
Response tokens stream into the UI one by one
Loading state shows 'Searching notes…' before first token arrives
A 429 response shows the rate limit message
Chat history clears on page reload (no persistence)
Compact mode fits within the landing hero panel column
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Landing page
tasks.description: Replace the 'Coming soon' placeholder in src/routes/+page.svelte. Create src/routes/+page.server.ts: load 4 stats (count of published notes, count of distinct topics, average word count across notes, total note citations served through chat via getTotalCitations() from notes.ts), and fetch the 3 most recent published notes. Layout per reference/UI/design_handoff_glass_atlas/Landing dark.png: top nav (from SCAFFOLD-03), two-column hero (left ~40%: large Literata serif headline 'Notes from a developer who would rather show his work.' + READ THE LATEST CTA button linking to /notes; right ~60%: Chat component in compact mode). The chat panel background in dark mode must use --color-surface-1 (#232019) — this is the fix; the design reference incorrectly shows a custom olive-green surface, use the standard token instead. Stats row below hero (4 stats). 'The latest field notes.' section with 3 NoteCard rows.
acceptance_criteria: Hero renders editorial headline and Chat compact panel side by side
Chat panel background uses --color-surface-1 in dark mode (not a custom token)
Stats row shows live counts from the database
3 most recent published notes appear as NoteCards below the stats
Both light and dark modes match the design reference
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: SEO meta tags + sitemap.xml
tasks.description: Update src/routes/+layout.svelte to add a <svelte:head> block with site-level meta: title ('Glass Atlas'), description, and Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:type, og:url). Update src/routes/notes/[slug]/+page.svelte to add per-note <svelte:head> with the note title and first sentence as description. Create src/routes/sitemap.xml/+server.ts: GET handler that queries all published notes, builds a sitemap XML string with <url> entries for /, /notes, and each /notes/[slug], and returns it with content-type application/xml.
acceptance_criteria: / has a <title> and og:title meta tag
/notes/[slug] has the note title as <title> and the first sentence as meta description
GET /sitemap.xml returns valid XML with entries for all published notes
npm run build passes with no errors

tasks.title: Notes text search
tasks.description: Extend listNotes() in src/lib/server/db/notes.ts to filter by q?: string — title ILIKE '%q%' OR any element of tags ILIKE '%q%' (case-insensitive; already specified in ADMIN-01a). Update src/routes/notes/+page.server.ts to forward the ?q= query parameter into listNotes(). Update src/routes/notes/+page.svelte: add a text search input to the existing filter bar; on change/submit navigate to /notes?q=<value> (preserve any active ?topic= param). When the page loads with ?focus=search in the URL, auto-focus the search input via a Svelte action or bind:this + $effect. Wire the nav search icon in src/lib/components/Nav.svelte to navigate to /notes?focus=search on click.
acceptance_criteria: ?q=<term> filters /notes to notes whose title or any tag matches (case-insensitive)
Empty state is shown when no notes match the search term
Clicking the nav search icon navigates to /notes?focus=search and focuses the search input
?q= and ?topic= params compose correctly — both can be active simultaneously
Clearing the search input navigates back to the unfiltered list
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Railway bucket upload flow (presigned, admin-only)
tasks.description: Implement first-party media upload for admin note editing using Railway Storage Buckets (RESOLVED-13). Add admin-only presign endpoint(s) that return short-lived signed upload URLs for direct browser upload. Enforce MIME allowlist: image/jpeg, image/png, image/svg+xml, image/gif, video/mp4. Wire admin note forms to upload files and write the resulting object key/URL into the existing `image` field. Keep bucket objects private; do not rely on permanent public bucket URLs.
acceptance_criteria: Admin-only upload endpoint returns short-lived signed upload URLs
Unsupported MIME types are rejected server-side before signing
Admin new/edit forms can upload a file and populate the cover media field from the upload result
Upload flow works for JPEG, PNG, SVG, GIF, and MP4
Bucket objects remain private; delivery path does not assume public bucket URLs
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Cover media type system — schema + migration + helpers
tasks.description: Implement the schema layer for the resolved media type scope (RESOLVED-14): JPEG, PNG, SVG, GIF, and MP4. Add a `media_type` text column to the `notes` table in schema.ts, defaulting to `image-jpeg`, accepting `image-jpeg` | `image-png` | `image-svg` | `image-gif` | `video-mp4`. Generate and run a Drizzle migration. Update CreateNoteInput and UpdateNoteInput types and all CRUD helpers in notes.ts (built in ADMIN-01b) to include `media_type`. Do not update UI components in this task — that is ADMIN-06b.
acceptance_criteria: schema.ts has a `media_type` text column on the notes table defaulting to `image-jpeg`
Migration applies cleanly against Neon without data loss
CreateNoteInput and UpdateNoteInput include `media_type`
All CRUD helpers in notes.ts pass `media_type` through correctly
npm run db:generate and npm run db:migrate succeed
npm run lint passes

tasks.title: Cover media type system — admin form + render dispatch
tasks.description: Wire the `media_type` field (added in ADMIN-06a) into the admin UI and public render components. Update the admin new and edit note forms to add a media type selector with the five options (image-jpeg, image-png, image-svg, image-gif, video-mp4) next to the cover media URL input. Update NoteCard.svelte to dispatch on `media_type`: render `` for image types and render `
acceptance_criteria: Admin form has a media type selector (image-jpeg, image-png, image-svg, image-gif, video-mp4) next to the cover media input
NoteCard renders `` for image types and a 16/9 `
Note detail renders cover media at the top of the main column, dispatched by media_type
All cover media containers enforce aspect-ratio: 16/9
Video cover media never autoplays
Notes with no cover media URL render correctly without a fallback image
npm run lint and npm run test:run pass

tasks.title: Implement light mode toggle + persistence
tasks.description: Add a working theme toggle to the global nav and theme application flow. Toggle must switch between light and dark explicitly, persist the user choice to localStorage, and apply the selected theme on initial load without flash. Ensure the toggle is available on public and admin pages, and the visual tokens for both themes are complete so no dark-only colors bleed into light mode.
acceptance_criteria: Theme toggle is visible in nav on all pages
Clicking toggle switches light/dark immediately
Selected theme persists across reloads via localStorage
Initial page load applies persisted theme before main content paints
Light mode and dark mode both render correctly on landing, notes list, note detail, and admin pages

tasks.title: Post-sign-in redirect to admin dashboard
tasks.description: Ensure authenticated admin sign-in lands on /admin by default in local and production OAuth flows. Implement explicit callback/redirect handling so successful auth does not strand the user on the generic sign-in page or landing route.
acceptance_criteria: Successful GitHub OAuth sign-in redirects to /admin when no explicit callbackUrl override is provided
Existing explicit callbackUrl behavior remains supported and is not overwritten
Unauthenticated visits to /admin still redirect to /auth/signin
Redirect behavior is covered by tests or documented reproducible verification steps

tasks.title: Signed-in admin navigation routes to /admin
tasks.description: Add signed-in admin routing affordances in the global navigation so authenticated admins can reach /admin directly from public pages. Hide admin entry points for unauthenticated visitors and ensure mobile/desktop nav parity.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated users see an Admin navigation entry that routes to /admin
Unauthenticated users do not see the Admin navigation entry
Navigation behavior is consistent across desktop and mobile layouts
Route and visibility behavior is validated with lint/tests and manual auth check

tasks.title: Theme-aware dropdown design system
tasks.description: Create a shared dropdown/select visual system that follows Glass Atlas theme tokens in both light and dark modes. Define explicit styling for trigger, menu surface, option text, hover/highlight, selected state, focus-visible ring, disabled state, and border treatments so dropdowns no longer inherit browser-default white popup surfaces in dark mode. Keep the component style aligned with the existing editorial UI language (sharp edges, strong lines, uppercase utility labels) and ensure token usage remains centralized in app-level styles rather than route-local one-offs.
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown trigger, content panel, and items are fully token-driven from current theme variables
Dark mode dropdown surfaces and option states never render white browser-default backgrounds
Focus, highlighted, selected, and disabled option states are visually distinct in both themes
The shared dropdown styles are implemented in reusable/global UI locations rather than duplicated route styles

tasks.title: Migrate native selects to custom themed Select
tasks.description: Replace existing native <select> controls in note browsing and admin note forms with the shared custom Select component so dropdown behavior and theming are consistent across the app. Preserve existing UX behavior and data wiring: notes filter query-param updates, admin create/edit form binding, and submitted values for category/status/mediaType fields. Ensure desktop and mobile layouts preserve current spacing and hierarchy after component substitution.
acceptance_criteria: Notes page topic and sort controls use the shared custom Select
Admin new/edit note category, status, and media type controls use the shared custom Select
Form submission and query-param behavior remain unchanged after migration
Dropdown interactions and visual states remain consistent on desktop and mobile breakpoints

tasks.title: Theme parity sweep for dropdown-adjacent controls
tasks.description: Run a targeted UI parity pass for dropdown-adjacent controls and menu-like surfaces after the Select migration, including any related trigger wrappers, filter bars, admin side panels, and nav-adjacent controls affected by theme toggle transitions. Fix residual mismatches where dark and light mode tokens do not fully propagate during initial load or live theme switches, and document any intentional browser/platform limitations if true native UI surfaces cannot be themed consistently.
acceptance_criteria: No obvious light-mode or dark-mode color mismatches remain around dropdown/menu-like controls on landing, notes, note detail, and admin screens
Theme toggle transitions do not leave stale surface/text colors on dropdown-related controls
Any unavoidable platform-specific styling limitations are recorded in conventions/docs with rationale
Validation includes lint/tests plus manual UI check notes for both themes

tasks.title: LLM review API for admin note editors
tasks.description: Implement optional admin critique streaming backend for both new and edit note flows. Add `src/lib/server/ai/review.ts` to build a compact structured critique prompt from `{ title, takeaway, body }` and call a free-tier OpenRouter model. Add `POST /api/admin/notes/review` in `src/routes/api/admin/notes/review/+server.ts` with admin auth enforcement, payload validation, SSE streaming response, and transparent upstream status handling for 429/503. This endpoint must not read from or write to the database and must remain non-blocking relative to save/publish flows.
acceptance_criteria: `POST /api/admin/notes/review` accepts JSON `{ title, takeaway, body }` and rejects invalid payloads with 400
Route is admin-only and returns 401 when unauthenticated
Endpoint streams critique via `ReadableStream`/SSE and never performs DB reads or writes
Upstream OpenRouter 429 and 503 statuses are forwarded to the client without remapping to generic 500
New server tests cover success path, payload validation, auth guard behavior, and 429/503 pass-through
`npm run test:run` and `npm run lint` pass

tasks.title: Admin editor Review UI integration (new + edit)
tasks.description: Integrate the optional manual Review experience into both admin note editors (`/admin/notes/new` and `/admin/notes/[slug]/edit`). Add a Review trigger and critique panel UX that POSTs current form state `{ title, takeaway, body }` to `/api/admin/notes/review`, streams compact structured critique output, and shows explicit loading/error states. Keep Save Draft/Create/Publish fully independent of critique success or failure.
acceptance_criteria: Both new and edit editors expose a manual Review action and render streamed critique output
Client sends `{ title, takeaway, body }` from current form state (including unsaved drafts)
Review failures (including 429/503) are shown as visible user-facing errors
Create/Save Draft/Publish flows remain unchanged and are never blocked by review
Client tests cover trigger behavior, streaming state transitions, and failure handling
`npm run test:run` and `npm run lint` pass

tasks.title: Admin markdown live-preview transform helper + tests
tasks.description: Implement a client-safe markdown preview transform used by admin editors: take raw `body`, apply wiki-link rendering against a provided resolved-slug set, and produce sanitized HTML for preview rendering. Keep this helper independent from server-only modules and include explicit fail-soft behavior contract (transform errors must not affect note editing/saving).
acceptance_criteria: Helper accepts markdown body plus resolved slugs and returns preview HTML suitable for right-pane rendering
Resolved `[[slug]]`/`[[slug|text]]` render as links; unresolved wiki-links render the missing-reference treatment
Preview helper preserves core markdown structures used in notes (headings, lists, emphasis, code fences, blockquotes, tables)
Unit tests cover wiki-link resolution, unresolved refs, markdown structure output, and transform failure handling contract
`npm run test:run` and `npm run lint` pass

tasks.title: MarkdownEditor split-pane UI with live typing preview
tasks.description: Upgrade `MarkdownEditor.svelte` from source-only mode to split-pane mode with a right preview pane that updates as the left CodeMirror content changes. Preserve existing bindable `value` behavior, keep update handling local to component state, and surface a non-blocking preview error state when preview rendering fails.
acceptance_criteria: Editor renders left CodeMirror source pane and right live preview pane in one split layout
Preview updates during typing without page navigation, form submit, or API requests
Preview failure state is visible but non-blocking; typing and bound value updates continue to work
Component styling works in light and dark themes and remains usable on mobile breakpoints
`npm run test:run` and `npm run lint` pass

tasks.title: Wire split-pane live preview into admin new/edit routes
tasks.description: Integrate the enhanced split-pane editor into both `/admin/notes/new` and `/admin/notes/[slug]/edit` flows, including loading the note slug/title list once per page request for wiki-link preview resolution. Ensure existing create/save draft/publish behavior remains unchanged and that preview concerns do not impact form action success.
acceptance_criteria: Both new and edit admin pages pass preload note-link data into `MarkdownEditor` for preview resolution
Live preview behavior is present on both pages and reflects unsaved body edits
Create note, Save Draft, and Publish actions continue using the same body payload semantics as before
No server-only module is imported into admin `.svelte` files for preview rendering logic
`npm run test:run`, `npm run check`, and `npm run lint` pass

tasks.title: Chunk embedding data model + retrieval helpers
tasks.description: Add a chunk-level retrieval data model for semantic RAG by introducing a `note_chunks` table in the `glass_atlas` schema, storing one embedding per section-aware chunk (vector(1536)), and implementing typed DB helpers for replacing a note’s chunks and searching top published chunks by cosine distance. Include suitable indexes for note-level maintenance and vector similarity reads.
acceptance_criteria: A new `note_chunks` table exists with note slug linkage, chunk ordering metadata, chunk text, and vector(1536) embeddings
Chunk retrieval helper returns top-N chunk rows ordered by pgvector cosine distance and scoped to published notes only
Chunk replace helper can upsert current chunks for a note and remove stale chunks from prior revisions
Migration and schema changes validate with existing project checks

tasks.title: Section-aware chunk generation + metadata-enriched embedding ingest
tasks.description: Implement section-aware chunking for note bodies, construct canonical embedding payloads that include note metadata (`title`, `category`, `tags`, `series`) plus chunk text, and update admin note save flows to regenerate chunk embeddings on create/update/publish with fail-soft behavior preserved when embedding calls fail.
acceptance_criteria: Chunker splits note content by section/paragraph boundaries with deterministic chunk ordering
Chunk embedding payload includes metadata context and chunk content in a stable template
Create note, Save Draft, and Publish actions regenerate chunk embeddings for the latest note body
Embedding failures do not block note persistence; failures are logged and chunk rows remain consistent

tasks.title: Chunk retrieval + hybrid prompt context assembly
tasks.description: Refactor chat context assembly to retrieve top similar chunks, group and cap them per note, and construct hybrid context blocks that combine note summary (`takeaway` fallback) with top chunk excerpt(s). Keep `/api/chat` request/response contract unchanged while preserving citation tracking and grounded-response behavior.
acceptance_criteria: `assembleContext()` uses chunk similarity retrieval rather than note-level body embeddings
Final prompt context remains compact and contains note summary plus retrieved chunk excerpt(s), not full note bodies
Citation slug reporting remains correct for retrieved notes
`POST /api/chat` behavior and streaming contract remain backward compatible

tasks.title: Persist anonymous chat quota counters by session hash
tasks.description: Refactor chat quota persistence to use an anonymous browser-session identifier hash in `chat_rate_limits` instead of IP identity. Add or adjust DB helper methods for atomic window reset and increment logic, and keep all identifiers opaque and non-PII.
acceptance_criteria: Quota counters are keyed by anonymous session hash, not raw IP.
Windowed counter logic supports reset after configured window.
No raw session token is persisted in DB.

tasks.title: Issue anonymous chat session cookie and enforce quota in /api/chat
tasks.description: Update `POST /api/chat` to read or issue a secure anonymous session cookie, enforce per-session quota before embeddings and LLM calls, and return 429 on threshold+1 while preserving SSE behavior for allowed requests.
acceptance_criteria: First request without cookie receives a new anonymous cookie.
Subsequent requests with the same cookie share one quota bucket across refresh.
Requests over limit return HTTP 429 before embedding or LLM work.
Allowed responses continue streaming via SSE.

tasks.title: Add route and utility tests for anonymous session quota
tasks.description: Replace IP-based quota tests with cookie-session coverage, including cookie issuance, persistence across requests, independent buckets per cookie, malformed or missing cookie fallback, and 429 boundary behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tests assert Set-Cookie behavior for first anonymous request.
Tests verify same-cookie accumulation and different-cookie isolation.
Tests verify 429 on limit+1 and window reset behavior.
No tests rely on shared global mutable state between cases.

tasks.title: Always-on lexical/topic retrieval + candidate fusion
tasks.description: Extend chat retrieval to run lexical/topic retrieval (title/tags/category) in parallel with semantic retrieval, then fuse and rerank a bounded candidate set for prompt assembly. Keep retrieval limits small and deterministic for latency control, and preserve published-note-only scope.
acceptance_criteria: Chat retrieval executes semantic and lexical/topic queries in parallel
Candidate fusion/rerank is deterministic and bounded by configured limits
Only published-note evidence is eligible for final prompt context
No full note-body injection is introduced during fusion
Unit tests cover lexical retrieval, fusion ordering, and empty-result behavior
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass

tasks.title: Confidence-gated insufficient-coverage fallback
tasks.description: Add confidence gating to chat answer orchestration so low-confidence evidence triggers a safe insufficient-coverage fallback instead of speculative direct answers. Preserve first-person voice and non-general-assistant behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Low-confidence retrieval paths return limited-coverage fallback behavior
Fallback behavior never fabricates unsupported claims
High-confidence paths continue to return grounded direct answers
Fallback decision is test-covered for boundary/edge cases
Route tests assert safe behavior for insufficient coverage
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass

tasks.title: Deterministic related-note link footer for fallback responses
tasks.description: Ensure insufficient-coverage responses include deterministic related-note links derived from retrieved slugs (not model-invented links), rendered with the italicized related-notes footer contract in chat output.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback responses include related-note links generated from retrieved slugs only
Invalid or unknown slugs are not emitted as clickable links
Italicized related-notes footer renders correctly in chat UI
Formatter and chat rendering tests cover link safety and output contract
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass

tasks.title: Numeric confidence tiers for chat retrieval
tasks.description: Replace the current non-empty-context confidence check with explicit semantic-distance tiers for chat retrieval. High-confidence chunks should allow the normal grounded answer path, borderline chunks should be flagged for limited-coverage handling, and low-confidence retrieval should skip the LLM and return the deterministic insufficient-coverage SSE fallback. Keep lexical/topic matches as supporting evidence and preserve published-note-only retrieval scope.
acceptance_criteria: Chat context assembly exposes enough confidence metadata for the route to distinguish high, borderline, and low-confidence retrieval
Clearly irrelevant semantic matches above the low-confidence distance cutoff do not reach the LLM
High-confidence retrieval continues to stream normal grounded answers
Confidence thresholds are centralized and documented in code comments or named constants
Unit and route tests cover high, borderline, low, and empty retrieval cases
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass

tasks.title: Limited-coverage prompt path and playful grounded voice
tasks.description: Tune the chat prompt contract so borderline-confidence retrieval still streams through the LLM with explicit limited-coverage instructions, while the main personality becomes more relaxed, informal, friendly, and lightly playful without weakening note grounding. Keep social-intent replies non-factual and ensure related-note footers remain deterministic from retrieved slugs.
acceptance_criteria: Borderline-confidence requests call the LLM with a limited-coverage instruction distinct from the normal answer path
The system prompt uses a relaxed and lightly playful first-person author voice while preserving strict grounding rules
Low-confidence requests still skip the LLM entirely
Social-intent replies remain templated and do not become factual question-answering
Tests assert the prompt shape for normal, borderline, low-confidence, and social paths
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass

tasks.title: Preserve embeddings on failed note reindex
tasks.description: Update the admin note save reindex contract so existing note-level and chunk-level embeddings are preserved when fresh embedding generation fails. Add the minimum schema/helper support needed to track last successful indexing state and detect when saved note content is newer than the semantic index. Keep note save/publish fail-soft and avoid partial chunk replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Failed note-level embedding generation no longer writes null over an existing embedding
Chunk rows are replaced only after all fresh chunk embeddings are generated successfully
Successful indexing records enough metadata to identify current versus stale semantic indexes
Failed indexing preserves the saved note content and previous vectors while recording stale/error state
Tests cover successful refresh, note-level embedding failure, and chunk embedding failure
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass

tasks.title: Show stale semantic index state in admin editors
tasks.description: Surface stale or failed semantic indexing state in the admin note editing experience after the preservation contract lands. The warning should be visible enough for the author to understand that chat retrieval may still use the last good vectors, while keeping save and publish actions available.
acceptance_criteria: Admin edit and relevant note list surfaces can show when a note's semantic index is stale or failed
The UI explains that the note content saved but chat retrieval may use the previous index
Healthy/current index state does not add noisy warnings
Save and publish actions remain available when stale-index warnings are shown
Tests cover stale/current display data or the server data shaping that drives the UI
npm run test:run and npm run lint pass
